pr.title: it: Matches for 5 functions in itdosei.c, partial matches for other 5
pr.author: Subarasheese

summary: Advanced decompilation of the Dosei item implementation by replacing several placeholder declarations in itdosei.c with C implementations and improving several remaining partial matches. The PR added five full matches and improved five other functions across Dosei animation, physics, and collision paths, with supporting header and item-var struct updates. Review focused on replacing raw item-offset access with the correct Item fields and item variable members where known.
what_changed: Implemented several previously stubbed or placeholder Dosei functions in src/melee/it/items/itdosei.c, including collision, animation, physics, and helper routines.

Added full C bodies for functions such as itDosei_UnkMotion0_Coll, itDosei_UnkMotion1_Anim, itDosei_UnkMotion1_Phys, itDosei_UnkMotion1_Coll, it_80281C6C, it_80282074, itDosei_UnkMotion5_Phys, itDosei_UnkMotion6_Coll, itDosei_UnkMotion7_Coll, and itDosei_UnkMotion10_Anim as shown in the diff excerpt.
Updated itDosei_ItemVars in itCommonItems.h: changed xDDC from s32 to f32, added xDF0 and xDF4 as f32 fields, and represented the 0xDF8 group as Vec3 xDF8.
Updated itdosei.h declarations to use concrete signatures for fn_80281734, it_80281C6C, and it_80282074 instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Added an itDoseiAttributes struct in itdosei.h with three f32 fields, used in itDosei_UnkMotion1_Phys to read x4_specialAttributes.
Added lb/lb_00B0.h include and extern declarations for float constants it_804DC874, it_804DC870, it_804DC878 and Item_80268E5C.
Follow-up PR comment states that raw pointer casting and M2C_FIELD usage were reduced by mapping known item offsets to struct fields such as ip->pos, ip->owner, xDD8, xDDC, xDE4, and xDF8.

Some unknown offsets or flag accesses, specifically 0x4CC and 0xDCB, were intentionally left as M2C_FIELD macros because they appeared to refer to CollData offsets or flag bits that were not yet safely named.
smart_moves: Moved Dosei per-item data modeling into itCommonItems.h instead of duplicating it in itdosei.h, reducing risk of conflicting struct definitions.
Converted xDDC to f32 and grouped the 0xDF8 data as a Vec3, enabling normal struct assignment instead of unsafe Vec3 pointer casts.
Used existing Item fields for known generic offsets, notably mapping offset 0x4C to ip->pos and offset 0x518 to ip->owner according to the author comment.
Kept uncertain offsets as M2C_FIELD rather than inventing field names, preserving match progress without creating misleading struct documentation.
Set animation frame speed both on the Item field x5D0_animFrameSpeed and through lb_8000BA0C, matching the visible pattern in several implemented functions.
Used GET_ITEM(gobj) or gobj->user_data consistently to access Item data, then assigned item vars through ip->xDD4_itemVar.dosei where known.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling item itemVar data, verify the true field types before replacing offset macros; here xDDC needed to be f32, not s32.
If three adjacent float fields represent vector data, modeling them as Vec3 can remove pointer casts and make collision-normal or position copies cleaner.
Reviewers prefer correct Item struct fields over raw offset casts when the field is known; a review comment specifically requested using the correct field of ip for the 0xDF8 Vec3 assignment.
Partial matches can still be improved safely by replacing only known magic offsets while leaving ambiguous CollData/flag offsets as M2C_FIELD until names are confirmed.
Header declarations should be upgraded from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once function signatures are known from source implementation.
For item physics functions, special attributes may require casting ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to an item-specific attributes type.
naming_conventions: Dosei item variables are accessed through ip->xDD4_itemVar.dosei.* members, retaining offset-derived names such as xDD8, xDDC, xDE4, xDF8 where semantic names are not yet known.
Item functions retain the project’s address/name convention such as it_80281C6C and it_80282074 when semantic names are unavailable.
Motion callbacks follow the existing item callback naming pattern itDosei_UnkMotionN_Anim, itDosei_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itDosei_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Item-specific attributes were named itDoseiAttributes with placeholder field names unk0, unk4, and unk8, reflecting weak semantic evidence.
Extern constants remain address-style names, for example it_804DC874, it_804DC870, and it_804DC878.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in implemented callbacks where stack padding was needed for matching.
Matched known item offset writes by substituting structured fields, including ip->pos, ip->owner, ip->x40_vel.x, ip->x5D0_animFrameSpeed, and ip->xDD4_itemVar.dosei fields.
Used Vec3 assignment for ip->xDD4_itemVar.dosei.xDE4 = ip->pos and ip->xDD4_itemVar.dosei.xDF8 = ip->x378_itemColl.floor.normal.
Preserved M2C_FIELD access for unresolved offsets 0x4CC and 0xDCB to avoid false naming while maintaining match behavior.
Used function pointer casts to HSD_GObjEvent in collision helper calls such as it_8026D62C and it_8026E8C4.
Reproduced animation-speed update patterns with ip->x5D0_animFrameSpeed and lb_8000BA0C(gobj->hsd_obj, speed).

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Let's use the correct field of `ip` here
review_feedback.context: The review targeted an implementation of itDosei_UnkMotion6_Coll that initially wrote to *(Vec3*)((u8*)ip + 0xDF8). The final approach modeled xDF8 in itDosei_ItemVars and assigned ip->xDD4_itemVar.dosei.xDF8 = ip->x378_itemColl.floor.normal.

review_feedback.author: Subarasheese
review_feedback.comment: Author follow-up said Item field usage was fixed and magic numbers were removed where known.
review_feedback.context: The author reported mapping xDD8, xDDC, xDE4, 0x4C, and 0x518 to structured fields, while leaving 0x4CC and 0xDCB as macros because their meanings were not clear enough.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for resolution or naming of remaining M2C_FIELD offsets 0x4CC and 0xDCB in Item or CollData.
Check whether itDoseiAttributes gained semantic field names beyond unk0, unk4, and unk8.
Look for subsequent matches in itdosei.c around itDosei_UnkMotion2_Anim, itDosei_UnkMotion2_Coll, itDosei_UnkMotion3_Anim, itDosei_UnkMotion9_Coll, and itDosei_UnkMotion11_Anim.
Verify whether extern declarations for Item_80268E5C and constants it_804DC870/874/878 were later moved to shared headers.
